title: Non-active-feature scribing stamp is legal from any phase; active-feature phase gate unchanged
read_first: skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs (handleStateScribingRun — the phase gate 'refused from phase' and the non-active-feature branch si-1 added)
decision for scribing-run-terminal-stamp (bee decisions search --text terminal-stamp)
action: In handleStateScribingRun, the phase gate (legal from swarming/reviewing/scribing) must apply ONLY when the named --feature matches the ACTIVE default-record feature (the run that updates state.last_scribing_run and produces the compounding transition). When --feature names a NON-active feature, skip the phase gate entirely: append the ledger line, touch nothing in the default record, succeed from any phase including compounding-complete and idle. Red-first (scoped): test in test_cli_state.mjs — fixture at phase compounding-complete, scribing-run --feature <other> succeeds with a ledger line and byte-unchanged state.json; active-feature run from compounding-complete still refuses with the existing message. Twins byte-identical; regen: onboard --apply, render_plugin, release_manifest --write, impact_registry --write.

truths: non-active stamp legal from every phase; ledger line written; default record byte-untouched
active-feature phase gate unchanged (still refuses from terminal)
twins byte-identical; lib_mirror, manifest, ledger parity green

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_state.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Non-active scribing-run stamp now legal from any phase (incl. terminal/idle); active-feature phase gate unchanged
